note_data.title: Quadratics
keyPoints: A **quadratic equation** has the general form ax² + bx + c = 0 where a ≠ 0
The **discriminant** Δ = b² - 4ac determines the nature of roots: Δ > 0 (two real distinct roots), Δ = 0 (two equal real roots), Δ < 0 (no real roots)
**Completing the square** transforms ax² + bx + c into a(x + p)² + q form where p = b/(2a) and q = c - b²/(4a)
The **quadratic formula** x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac))/(2a) gives exact roots when factoring is not possible
**Factorization** requires finding two numbers that multiply to give 'ac' and add to give 'b'
The **sum of roots** α + β = -b/a and **product of roots** αβ = c/a for equation ax² + bx + c = 0
The **axis of symmetry** of parabola y = ax² + bx + c is x = -b/(2a)
**Maximum or minimum value** occurs at x = -b/(2a); minimum when a > 0, maximum when a < 0
A quadratic function can be written as **y = a(x - h)² + k** where (h, k) is the vertex

definitions.definition: The expression b² - 4ac which determines the nature and number of real roots of the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0
The process of rewriting a quadratic expression ax² + bx + c in the form a(x + p)² + q
The values of x for which the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0 is satisfied
The turning point of a parabola, located at (-b/(2a), f(-b/(2a)))

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the discriminant value or nature of roots directly without working
Show complete method including substitution and simplification
Use the previous result directly in your next step
Start with LHS and manipulate to reach RHS, or start with given result and verify

modelAnswer.question: The equation 2x² - 8x + k = 0 has equal roots. Find the value of k.
modelAnswer.answer:
For equal roots, discriminant = 0
b² - 4ac = 0
(-8)² - 4(2)(k) = 0
64 - 8k = 0
8k = 64
k = 8
markBreakdown: Mark 1: States discriminant = 0 for equal roots
Mark 3: Solves correctly to get k = 8

commonMistakes: Never say 'no solutions' for negative discriminant — always say 'no real roots'
Students often forget the ± symbol when using the quadratic formula
Writing (x + p)² instead of (x - p)² when completing the square with vertex form
Confusing sum and product of roots formulas: sum is -b/a, not b/a
Not checking that a ≠ 0 when identifying quadratic equations
examinerTips: Always check your discriminant calculation as this appears in most quadratic questions
When completing the square, show each step clearly for method marks
Quadratic inequalities often follow quadratic equations in Paper 1
Remember to give exact answers unless told to round
formulas: Vertex form: y = a(x - h)² + k
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Parabola showing vertex at (-b/(2a), minimum/maximum value), axis of symmetry as vertical line x = -b/(2a), and x-intercepts as roots of the quadratic equation
